license_note: Confirmed via CrossRef DOI metadata: dx.doi.org/10.17504/protocols.io.dsd6a5
title: Extracting DNA from viruses embedded in agarose
author: Grieg F. Steward and Alexander I. Culley

steps_raw.instruction: Add agarose to SE buffer for a final concentration of 1.5% (wt/vol).
Melt the agarose in a microwave oven, then cool and maintain at 37°C in a water bath.

Warm the viral concentrate to 37°C in the water bath, then immediately mix with an equal volume of molten agarose and quickly transfer the mixture to casting molds. Casting in rectangular plug molds is preferred if the embedded DNA is to be analyzed by electrophoresis, since this results in plugs of uniform height and thickness that fit the wells without extensive trimming. Electrophoresis using plugs that are not uniform will result in bands with uneven intensity. Special rectangular plug molds for PFGE are available from BioRad. Alternatively, one can draw the molten mixture into a 1-cc syringe that has had the tip cut off, or simply pipette the mixture as drops onto a sheet of plastic wrap or Parafilm M ® .

Once the agarose has gelled, transfer the plug (or noodle from the syringe, or buttons from the parafilm), into a tube containing 5 volumes of lysis buffer amended with proteinase K (1 mg mL –1 final concentration).
Decant the lysis buffer, being careful not to lose the plugs.

Optional: Rinse the plugs twice, each time adding 25 volumes of fresh TE containing 1 mM PMSF, incubating for 1 h with gentle agitation, then decanting the rinse fluid. 01:00:00 This step will inactivate the proteinase K, which is recommended if the DNA in the plug is to be further manipulated with enzymes (e.g., digestion with restriction endonucleases).
Rinse the plugs, add 50 volumes of fresh TE with no PMSF, incubate for 30 min. with gentle agitation, then decant the rinse fluid. 00:30:00
Once more, rinse the plugs, add 50 volumes of fresh TE with no PMSF, incubate for 30 min. with gentle agitation, then decant the rinse fluid. 00:30:00
Store the plugs at 4°C submerged in TE.
